item.title: Foundering: The True Story of Bob Lee’s Killer
item.description: San Francisco officials were eager to quash the doom loop narrative and insisted that Bob Lee was killed by someone he knew. But Lee’s friends and family had never heard of his accused murderer, Nima Momeni.

item.title: Tax Refund Splurge for Many Americans Is Paying Down Debt
item.description: Households use this year’s bigger refunds to trim debt, pay bills — and treat themselves with what’s left.

item.title: Foundering: Tech Executive’s Mysterious Death Prompts San Francisco Doom Loop Panic
item.description: When Bob Lee, the founder of Cash App, was killed in the streets of San Francisco, his death was initially a mystery. The city took the blame.

item.title: NBA Basketball League in Europe Draws Private Credit Lenders
item.description: Private capital heavyweights including Apollo Global Management, Ares Management Corp. and Sixth Street Partners are in early discussions to help fund the National Basketball Association’s expansion into Europe, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Investors Aren’t Buying Into Policymakers’ Gloom
item.description: Officials gathered in Washington say markets are too buoyant.

item.title: AI Trade Fuels Emerging-Markets Stock Rally After TSMC Results
item.description: A rally in technology stocks fueled a third day of gains for emerging-market equities after an upbeat forecast from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. bolstered confidence in earnings prospects for the sector.

item.title: ASML’s Premium to US-Listed Rivals Shrinks to Decade Low
item.description: ASML Holding NV’s valuation relative to its US-listed peers has hit a decade low, suggesting that the Dutch chip-equipment maker’s rally may still have room to run.

item.title: Blackrock’s Turner Sees Bond Opportunity From Mispriced ECB Bets
item.description: Markets’ mispricing of European Central Bank interest-rate hikes is creating an opportunity to buy shorter-dated euro-area bonds, according to Blackrock Inc.

item.title: Russia Looks to Boost Wealth Fund as Soon as May on Oil Windfall
item.description: Russia may start rebuilding its sovereign wealth fund as soon as next month from windfall oil gains following the surge in crude prices over the Middle East war.

item.title: Anthropic Plans to Bring Mythos to UK Banks Within the Next Week
item.description: Anthropic PBC is planning to release its closely watched Mythos artificial intelligence model to UK financial institutions in the coming week.

item.title: Debt-Laden Bahrain’s Bonds Bounce Back From Shock of Iran War
item.description: Bahrain was already having a tough time before the Iran war broke out.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Was Quicker Than Thought in March at 2.6%
item.description: Euro-zone inflation was faster than initially reported in March, suggesting stronger upward pressure on prices from the Iran war.

item.title: Sinking Gas Exports Leave Cash-Strapped Bolivia Needing IMF Deal
item.description: Bolivia is low on cash after making more than $500 million in debt payments last month, increasing the urgency of reaching a deal with the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: HalkBank-DOJ Deal to End Criminal Case Questioned by Lawmakers
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to drop a long-running US criminal case against a Turkish state-owned bank for allegedly violating US sanctions on Iran is being questioned by Democratic lawmakers.

item.title: Pilot in Deadly Plane Crash Was Being Filmed for Apple Vision Pro Video
item.description: Claire Lomas, a British paraplegic known for overcoming medical trauma to participate in the London Marathon, was being filmed for an Apple Inc. immersive Vision Pro video when she crashed an aircraft in the Jordan desert two years ago.

item.title: KKR and Apollo Among Potential Suitors for Portugal’s Logoplaste
item.description: US private equity firms KKR & Co. and Apollo Global Management are considering bids for Portuguese packaging maker Logoplaste, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Equinor Trading Profits to Beat Forecast as War Spurs Volatility
item.description: Equinor ASA said first-quarter earnings from its marketing, midstream and processing business will exceed its guidance of about $400 million, with the Middle East conflict driving “significant volatility.”

item.title: Pakistan Boosts Mediation Efforts as US, Iran Weigh Longer Truce
item.description: Pakistan stepped up efforts to ensure the US and Iran prolong a ceasefire that’s set to end next week, allowing more time for the warring sides to negotiate a lasting peace deal.

item.title: Half-Full Tanker Heading to Japan Highlights Scramble for Oil
item.description: A partially filled supertanker heading to Japan after receiving its Middle Eastern oil cargo via a ship transfer at sea highlights the desperate measures the nation’s refiners are taking to secure supply quickly.

item.title: Brad Setser on the War in Iran and the Future of the US Dollar
item.description: How This Shock Differs From the 1970s.

item.title: Odd Lots: Setser on the Future of the US Dollar (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Setser on the Future of the US Dollar (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Setser on the Future of the US Dollar (Podcast)】

item.title: EasyJet Shares Slump Amid Warning of Loss Driven By Iran War
item.description: EasyJet Plc shares fell the most in almost four years after the UK budget airline warned of a loss in the first half year, caused by the disruption from the Iran war.

item.title: Stellantis to Invest €100 Million to Keep Plant Near Paris Open
item.description: Stellantis NV will invest €100 million ($118 million) to transform its Poissy car-assembly site near Paris, unveiling a plan to keep the plant open as the maker Peugeots and Citroëns scales back its European manufacturing operations.

item.title: Inditex Flags Contractor Data Leak, Says Client Records Safe
item.description: Zara owner Inditex SA disclosed a data breach at an outside firm, saying intruders gained access to information on commercial relations at the world’s biggest clothing firm.

item.title: Singapore Rejects US Claims in Manufacturing, Forced Labor Probe
item.description: Singapore’s business lobby has joined the government in pushing back against a sweeping US trade probe that alleges manufacturing overcapacity and failures to enforce bans on imports linked to forced labor.

item.title: Barry Callebaut Sees Profit Falling After Sharp Cocoa-Price Drop
item.description: Swiss chocolate maker Barry Callebaut AG said profit will decline this year, cutting its outlook after a sharp drop in cocoa prices.

item.title: Abu Dhabi Fund Led by Crown Prince Inks $2.3 Billion Jordan Deal
item.description: A newly created wealth fund overseen by Abu Dhabi’s crown prince has signed a $2.3 billion pact for a railway project in Jordan, highlighting the role the entity is likely to play in advancing the emirate’s strategic relationships.

item.title: TotalEnergies Flags Strong Quarter as War Rattles Energy Market
item.description: TotalEnergies SE signaled a strong first quarter as surging energy prices and rising production outside the Middle East helped offset the impact of the Iran war.

item.title: CATL Overtakes PetroChina in Market Value as Green Bets Ramp Up
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. has once again overtaken oil giant PetroChina Co. in market value, as investors pile into the battery maker on war-driven optimism over green energy.

item.title: Investors Look Past War to Focus on Fundamentals, Yardeni Says
item.description: Wall Street veteran Ed Yardeni said investors are looking past the war in the Middle East and focusing on market fundamentals.
